old_claim.note: Gunpowder provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: The corrected node is scoped to early modern oceanic exploration and route-finding; gunpowder warfare belongs on conquest or military-expansion nodes, not as a direct prerequisite for navigation.

source_shape.source_locator: Article body: describes the caravel as a 15th- to 17th-century Spanish and Portuguese long-voyage ship developed for African coastal exploration, without making gunpowder a prerequisite.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source pins the scoped exploration claim to ship design and oceanic voyaging rather than military technology.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Keeping gunpowder laundered colonial conquest into the navigation node; the corrected scope keeps warfare dependencies on separate military or imperial nodes.

ontology_before: The graph allowed a military technology to be a direct prerequisite of the broad exploration node.
ontology_after: The graph separates oceanic route-finding from gunpowder-enabled conquest or coercion.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Graph invariant 2026-06-20-exploration-global-oceanic-scope keeps gunpowder absent as a direct prerequisite.
would_reject_if: The node were rescoped to armed colonial conquest rather than oceanic exploration.
A source showed gunpowder was a direct prerequisite for the first early modern oceanic route-finding voyages.
short_reason: Gunpowder is military context, not a direct prerequisite for the scoped oceanic exploration node.
